name: AZQ Stage 1 Wave A Checks
purpose: Define task-specific automated checks for Stage 1 Wave A so the Codex runner can evaluate patches without hardcoding all task logic.

baseline_checks.name: compileall
baseline_checks.description: Verify the azq package still compiles after the patch.

baseline_checks.name: storage_import
baseline_checks.description: Verify the new Finis storage module imports cleanly.

human_checks: No existing Finis command handlers were refactored yet.
The patch is limited to storage module scaffolding and tiny import-safe changes only.

STAGE1-WAVEA-02.description: Add canonical Finis path constants and directory helpers.

checks.name: path_helpers_smoke
checks.description: Verify storage path helpers exist and return stable-looking paths.
checks.code:
from azq.finis import storage
assert hasattr(storage, 'GOALS_DIR') or hasattr(storage, 'FINIS_GOALS_DIR')
assert hasattr(storage, 'GOALS_JSON') or hasattr(storage, 'LEGACY_GOALS_JSON')

human_checks: All Finis path ownership now lives in azq/finis/storage.py.
Directory helper behavior is deterministic and readable.

STAGE1-WAVEA-03.description: Define the canonical normalized goal schema.

checks.name: goal_normalization_schema
checks.description: Verify a legacy-shaped goal normalizes into the canonical Stage 1 schema.

human_checks: Legacy goal -> title mapping is explicit.
Fallback behavior for missing fields is conservative, not destructive.

STAGE1-WAVEA-04.description: Implement markdown goal file serialization and parsing.

checks.name: goal_round_trip
checks.description: Verify a canonical goal record can round-trip through markdown serialization and parsing.
checks.code:
from azq.finis import storage
record = {
    'goal_id': 'FINIS_001',
    'title': 'Round trip test',
    'status': 'active',
    'created': '2026-01-01T00:00:00',
    'description': 'desc',
    'derived_from': ['spark_001']
}
serializer = getattr(storage, 'serialize_goal_record', None) or getattr(storage, 'goal_to_markdown', None)
parser = getattr(storage, 'parse_goal_record', None) or getattr(storage, 'goal_from_markdown', None)
assert serializer is not None, 'serializer not found'
assert parser is not None, 'parser not found'
text = serializer(record)
parsed = parser(text)
for field in ['goal_id', 'title', 'status', 'created', 'description', 'derived_from']:
    assert parsed[field] == record[field], f'round-trip mismatch: {field}'

human_checks: The markdown format is human-readable and diff-friendly.
The file format is simple enough for future Formam code to consume.

STAGE1-WAVEA-05.description: Implement file-backed goal repository reads and id allocation.

checks.name: repository_read_helpers_exist
checks.description: Verify file-backed read helpers and next-id allocation helpers exist.
checks.code:
from azq.finis import storage
assert any(hasattr(storage, name) for name in ['load_goal', 'load_goal_by_id']), 'missing single-goal loader'
assert any(hasattr(storage, name) for name in ['load_goals', 'load_all_goals']), 'missing all-goals loader'
assert any(hasattr(storage, name) for name in ['next_goal_id', 'get_next_goal_id']), 'missing next-id helper'

human_checks: Exact lookup uses exact goal_id matching.

STAGE1-WAVEA-06.description: Add read-only legacy goals.json migration reader.

checks.name: legacy_reader_exists
checks.description: Verify a dedicated legacy JSON reader exists.
checks.code:
from azq.finis import storage
assert any(hasattr(storage, name) for name in ['load_legacy_goals_json', 'load_legacy_goals']), 'missing legacy reader'

checks.name: legacy_reader_missing_file_behavior
checks.description: Verify the legacy reader handles missing goals.json cleanly.
checks.code:
from pathlib import Path
from azq.finis import storage
reader = getattr(storage, 'load_legacy_goals_json', None) or getattr(storage, 'load_legacy_goals', None)
assert reader is not None
missing = Path('this_file_should_not_exist_12345.json')
try:
    reader(missing)
except FileNotFoundError:
    pass
except TypeError:
    # acceptable if reader does not take a path override yet
    pass

human_checks: Legacy parsing is clearly separated from canonical file-backed reads.
No code in this task writes back to goals.json.
